name: Evidently AI
what_it_does: Open-source ML and LLM evaluation and monitoring framework with a cloud API that generates data quality, drift, and model performance reports — enabling agents to evaluate datasets, detect distribution shift, and monitor ML and LLM systems in production.
use_cases: Generating data drift reports comparing training and production feature distributions to detect covariate shift
Running LLM output quality checks (toxicity, sentiment, semantic similarity, hallucination) via the Evidently cloud API
Scheduling automated monitoring snapshots that track model performance metrics over rolling time windows
Comparing dataset quality between data pipeline runs to catch upstream data issues before they reach the model
Building custom monitoring dashboards using Evidently's report API output to feed team observability tools
not_for: Real-time streaming monitoring requiring sub-second alerting — Evidently is batch-oriented and works on data snapshots
Annotation or labeling workflows — Evidently is for evaluation and monitoring, not data collection
Teams needing fully managed enterprise SLA with dedicated support — the open-source version requires self-management
best_when: You need a flexible, open-source-first framework for evaluating ML and LLM outputs with deep support for statistical tests, drift metrics, and custom scorers — especially when you want to run evaluations locally or self-hosted.
avoid_when: You need real-time production alerting with minimal infrastructure — Evidently requires data snapshots and report generation which is batch-oriented, not event-driven.

auth.notes: Evidently Cloud uses API key authentication. Open-source self-hosted version requires no auth. Cloud API key is workspace-scoped with no granular permissions. Set via environment variable or SDK init.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open-source Python library is fully free with no limits. Evidently Cloud free tier includes limited report storage and snapshots.
pricing.paid_tiers: Evidently Cloud Pro and Enterprise: custom pricing with extended retention, team features, and SLA
pricing.notes: The Python library (Apache 2.0) is the primary product and is completely free. Evidently Cloud is an optional hosted UI for storing and sharing reports. Most users get full value from the open-source library alone.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits on open-source library (local execution). Cloud API has undocumented soft limits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python library raises descriptive exceptions with clear messages. Cloud API returns standard HTTP errors. Most common errors relate to data schema mismatches which can be cryptic.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Report generation is deterministic given the same input data — rerunning produces the same output. Cloud snapshot uploads are idempotent by snapshot ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Report generation runs in-process (not via API call) for the OSS library — agents must handle potentially slow pandas/numpy computation blocking the event loop
Data schemas between reference and current datasets must exactly match — column name or type mismatches raise opaque errors
LLM evaluators in Evidently call external LLM APIs (OpenAI, etc.) — agents must provision those API keys separately
Cloud snapshot storage requires Evidently Cloud account even when using the OSS library for computation
Large datasets cause memory issues in the Python library — agents processing production-scale data should sample or use chunked evaluation

security_score_components.security_notes: Open-source library processes data locally — no data leaves the agent environment. Cloud version uses HTTPS. Apache 2.0 license allows full code audit. SOC 2 for cloud tier. Self-hosted gives complete data sovereignty.
